input.user_input: My client is planning to acquire a competitor. How do I assess whether the deal will trigger HSR antitrust filing thresholds?

actual:
To determine whether your client’s proposed acquisition triggers a filing requirement under the Hart-Scott-Rodino (HSR) Antitrust Improvements Act, you must conduct a multi-step analysis. The HSR Act requires parties to notify the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) and the Department of Justice (DOJ) before completing certain mergers and acquisitions, followed by a mandatory waiting period.

Because the FTC adjusts the jurisdictional thresholds annually based on changes in the gross national product (usually effective in late February or early March), you must always verify the thresholds in effect at the time the transaction will close. 

Here is the standard framework for assessing HSR reportability:

### 1. Identify the Ultimate Parent Entities (UPEs)
HSR analysis is not limited to the direct buyer and target. You must identify the "Ultimate Parent Entity" (UPE) of both the acquiring person and the acquired person. The UPE is the entity at the top of the corporate chain that is not "controlled" by any other entity. Control is generally
